subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative with logical progression through motivation, derivation, data, modelling, and conclusion
Wellâdefined structure with table of contents, numbered subsections and consistent headings
Mostly concise content with focus on core mathematical objectives
weaknesses: Minor lapses in logical flow (abrupt figures, copyâpaste terminology error) interrupt reader engagement
Some tables misaligned and equations spill into text, detracting from presentation polish
Excessive environmental commentary and bibliography inconsistencies add unnecessary length
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently place the work at band 3 for coherence and structure, with just one strand at band 2 for conciseness. The exploration is coherent and wellâstructured, though minor lapses prevent the top band. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects this performance.

strengths: Mostly consistent and correct use of integral notation, dy/dx, Î£ and Ï symbols
Effective combination of equations, graphs, tables, and verbal explanations
Clear stepâbyâstep logical structure that a competent reader can follow
weaknesses: Occasional notation errors (extra âdxâ, missing exponent in distance formula) and missing units
Axis labels and scales are sometimes omitted, reducing clarity
Nonâstandard symbols (Ï, Î½) and minor algebraic sloppiness in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit firmly in band 3, with generally effective mathematical language, representation, and logical clarity, despite some recurring minor errors. A holistic score of 3/4 best matches this level of performance.

strengths: Independent choice of a household container and manual coordinate collection shows genuine initiative
Personal motivation (environmental label waste) is clearly linked to the investigation
Use of Geogebra for scaling demonstrates technical initiative
weaknesses: Techniques employed (Lagrange interpolation, image scaling) are standard rather than highly innovative
Personal angle, while clear, does not push into exceptional territory
No exploration of alternative unconventional methodologies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With all three strands at band 2, the candidate shows a good level of independent thinking and personal approach, but not outstanding originality. A score of 2/3 accurately reflects this solid yet not exceptional engagement.

strengths: Meaningful identification of modelling errors and suggestion of general improvements
Some recognition of rounding and fit limitations
weaknesses: Limited depth in analysis of numerical results (no plausibility check)
Evaluation of outcomes is cursory, lacking quantitative error margins or future implications
Reflection is intermittent rather than sustained or critically insightful
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (depth and critical analysis) align with band 2, but the evaluation of outcomes is only band 1, and overall reflection lacks the depth to fully merit band 2. A conservative score of 1/3 best represents this uneven performance.

strengths: Relevant integral calculus, arcâlength derivation, polynomial interpolation and numerical integration effectively address the aim
Techniques are commensurate with SL level and applied in context with correct domains
Derivation of the surfaceâarea formula and step-by-step integral computations are methodical
weaknesses: Several algebraic and notation errors (missing squares, extra âdxâ) detract from accuracy
Reliance on online calculators for polynomial fits reduces evidence of manual understanding
Assumptions about polynomial profiles lack validation through residual or error analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands achieve band 4 (relevance, level appropriateness) and one strand sits at band 3 (understanding and accuracy). Averaging these performances yields a band-3 score of 3/6, reflecting effective but imperfect mathematical application.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are laid out in a step-by-step sequence, and working is shown for each section. Occasional messy algebra (section F derivative) blurs clarity, but the logical thread is still clear to a competent reader, matching the âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ band.

Derivations and integrals show good conceptual understanding; most results follow logically. Nevertheless, several transcription errors (extra dx terms, inconsistent derivative in Section F) and absence of units in the final answer indicate only âgood understanding with minor errorsâ, corresponding to band 3.

Equations, graphs (Geogebra screenshots, coloured points), tables of coordinates and verbal explanation are all present and support one another. These representations are generally well chosen and effective, though axis labels and scales are not always visible, so the use is good rather than exemplary.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: motivation â derivation of the formula â data collection â modelling â calculation â limitations â conclusion. Transitions between these sections are explicit and the reader can follow the mathematical thread without major jumps. Minor lapses in logic (e.g., suddenly quoting figures without explanation, some derivative notation errors) slightly interrupt the flow, preventing the âexcellentâ descriptor but still demonstrating solid coherence and logical development throughout.

Using image scaling, manual point plotting and section-by-section modelling shows good creativity and initiative. Nonetheless, these are well-known methods in IA work; nothing exceptionally novel is introduced, so the work aligns with the âgood level of creativity and initiativeâ descriptor.

There is thoughtful analysis of the modelling method and acknowledgement of rounding effects. However, critical scrutiny of the numerical results (e.g., plausibility of 995 cmÂ² for a 24 cm tall bottle) is limited. The analysis surpasses âbasicâ but falls short of âdeep and insightfulâ, hence band 2.

The student briefly states that the result will help size labels and mentions material savings, but there is no quantitative comparison or discussion of accuracy versus real measurements. This constitutes only a limited evaluation of outcomes, matching the band-1 descriptor.

Reflection appears in the âWeaknesses and Limitationsâ section and intermittently in earlier explanations. The student recognises key sources of error (point placement, rounding, model fit) and suggests practical improvements, showing meaningful but not deep, sustained reflectionâappropriate to band 2.

Throughout, the student employs correct integral notation, uses dy/dx, Î£ and Ï properly, and labels units. In a few places the derivative is written with an extraneous âdxâ or a squared term is omitted, so accuracy and consistency are not perfect; nevertheless, appropriate mathematical language is used in most parts, satisfying the âmostly consistentâ descriptor.

The investigation is clearly personalâmotivated by reducing label waste at homeâand the candidateâs own photographs and measurements drive the modelling process. This âclear evidence of personal approachâ corresponds to band 2, while the approach is not so innovative as to merit the top band.

Most content is relevant to determining the surface area, yet extended environmental commentary, a full re-derivation of the arc-length formula and repeated screenshots of standard graphs add unnecessary length. These digressions mean the work is âmostlyâ rather than âwhollyâ concise, in line with the band-2 descriptor.

Integral calculus for surfaces of revolution, arc-length derivation, Lagrange interpolation and numerical integration are all relevant and used effectively to address the aim. Minor algebraic slips do not undermine relevance. The breadth and appropriateness meet the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ level.

The student independently selected a household container, collected coordinates, and chose Lagrange interpolation rather than relying on software regression. These choices show significant independent thinking, though they are standard techniques and not especially novel, fitting band 2.

A table of contents, numbered subsections and consistent headings give the piece an unmistakable structure that is easy to navigate. Intro-body-conclusion are present, and each body subsection deals with a distinct stage of the investigation. However, some tables are mis-aligned and several equations spill into surrounding text, signalling that organisation, while good, is not âexceptionallyâ polished.

All mathematics is clearly HL-level (calculus, polynomial interpolation). Techniques are applied in context with correct domains and interpretations, demonstrating work âfully commensurate with course levelâ. Occasional simplification by online calculators slightly lowers the impression but not enough to drop below band 4.

annotations.comment: The table of contents lacks page references, which hinders navigation. Consider adding consistent page numbers for each section.
The introduction effectively connects personal motivation to environmental context, showing clear engagement with the problem.
The environmental commentary, while interesting, digresses from the mathematical aim. Consider condensing to focus on your calculation objectives.
Citing Dawkins without a full reference makes sourcing unclear. Include a properly formatted bibliography entry here.
The expression $$\Delta s = \sqrt{(x_1 - x_0)^2 + (y_1 - y_0)}$$ omits the square on the y-difference term. It should read $$\sqrt{(x_1 - x_0)^2 + (y_1 - y_0)^2}\,$$.
The step-by-step algebraic manipulation to the limit process is thorough and shows strong understanding of arc-length integration.
The derivation of the surface area integral formula is precise and correctly uses $$\int_a^b 2\pi y\sqrt{1+(\frac{dy}{dx})^2}\,dx$$.
Figures 2 and 3 lack axis labels and scales, which reduces clarity of the approximations. Label axes to improve multiple representation effectiveness.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting. Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) for uniform presentation.
The substitution into the surface area formula incorrectly writes $$\sqrt{1+(f'(x))}$$ without squaring. The integrand should include $$\sqrt{1+(f'(x))^2}\,$$.
Using Geogebra to scale and plot points demonstrates initiative and significantly reduces manual error.
Excluding the base and top from the surface calculation needs justification. How will this affect the total area you report?
The Lagrange interpolation formula is presented correctly and labels are clear, showing effective use of mathematical notation.
Assuming all container sections follow polynomial profiles should be justified. Consider validating the choice with residual analysis or fit error.
Figure 6âs segmentation boundaries are not explicitly annotated in the image. Add labels for sections AâH directly on the diagram for clarity.
In Section A, the domain is miswritten as $$0\le y\le 2.2$$ instead of $$0\le x\le 2.2$$, which may confuse readers.
Referring to the container as a âceramic potâ in Section B seems to be a copyâpaste error. Correct terminology to maintain coherence.
Relying on an online calculator for polynomial fits without showing any manual verification reduces the demonstration of understanding.
Table 3 uses symbols Ï and Î½ which are unexplained and nonstandard here. Use x and y headings or clarify these symbols.
In the surface area calculation (page 11), the derivative notation $$\frac{dy}{dx}$$ is followed by âdxâ, which is incorrect. Remove the extra âdxâ.
The calculus integral steps to compute each sectionâs area are methodical and show effective use of relevant mathematics.
Several intermediate sums lack units (e.g., 54.07), which may confuse the cumulative result. Include cmÂ² in each result.
The calculation for Section F on page 14 is truncated and unreadable. Ensure full equations are displayed and proofs are complete.
The weaknesses section acknowledges human error but lacks quantitative impact analysis. Consider estimating the error margin in cmÂ².
The conclusion restates results but could reflect on precision by comparing with direct measurement of the containerâs label.
